unit.name: Scaled dot-product attention, keys and values, and cross-attention
unit.description: Generalizes attention into a scalable matrix-based layer using scaled dot products, multiple queries in parallel, and learned key/value projections.
unit.summary: The attention operator is simplified by replacing an arbitrary learned similarity function with a dot product, then improved by scaling that dot product by the square root of the vector dimension to avoid softmax saturation and vanishing gradients as dimensions grow [ts=1932s][ts=2010s][ts=2041s]. The operator is then vectorized to process many queries in parallel: similarities between a query matrix and data vectors are computed by matrix multiplication, softmax is applied across the data dimension for each query, and outputs are formed by another matrix multiplication that takes weighted combinations of data vectors [ts=2160s][ts=2202s][ts=2275s]. The lecture then separates two roles previously played by the same data vectors by introducing keys for matching and values for retrieval, both obtained by learned projections of the data vectors [ts=2297s][ts=2352s][ts=2417s][ts=2428s]. This yields a stand-alone cross-attention layer in which query vectors and data vectors may come from different sources, such as source and target sequences or image features and caption tokens [ts=2696s][ts=2714s][ts=2737s].

key_points.text: The simplest similarity function for attention is a dot product between vectors.
Scaled dot-product attention divides by sqrt(D) to keep softmax gradients from collapsing as vector dimension grows.
Multiple query vectors can be processed in parallel by computing all pairwise similarities with a matrix multiply.
Keys are used for matching against queries, while values are the vectors linearly combined to produce the output.
Learned projections compute keys and values from data vectors: K = XW_K and V = XW_V.
Cross-attention uses separate query vectors and data vectors, potentially coming from different sources.

kp_catalog.name: Scaled dot-product attention
kp_catalog.description: Using a dot product as similarity and scaling it by the square root of the feature dimension stabilizes softmax behavior and gradient flow. Attention scores are divided by a dimension-based constant so large dot products do not saturate the softmax and kill useful gradients.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because scaled dot-product attention clarifies using a dot product as similarity and scaling it by the square root of the feature dimension stabilizes softmax behavior and gradient flow, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices though importance labels split between high and medium.

kp_catalog.name: Keys and values
kp_catalog.description: Attention separates matching and retrieval by projecting data vectors into keys used for similarity computation and values used for the weighted output.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because keys and values clarifies attention separates matching and retrieval by projecting data vectors into keys used for similarity computation and values used for the weighted output, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Encoder-decoder cross-attention
kp_catalog.description: At each decoding step, compute scores between the decoder state and encoder states, normalize them with softmax, form a context vector, and combine it with the decoder state to predict the next token. Cross-attention uses one set of query vectors to retrieve information from a separate set of data vectors, often from another modality or sequence.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because encoder-decoder cross-attention formalizes how at each decoding step, compute scores between the decoder state and encoder states, normalize them with softmax, form a context vector, and combine it with the decoder state to predict the next token, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.
